_human_review.notes: Project owner instructed Codex to mark the SA Stage B generated/reconciled tests as approved for internal/project benchmarking after manual review on 2026-06-05. This is not legal advice, external counsel review, or procurement-grade legal approval; candidate labels remain separate from locked recall baselines.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: SA Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.
_strict_reconciliation.strict_source: PreSendReviewEngine.review(..., review_profile='strict') after narrowed SA/US Stage B detector precision fixes

must_detect.matched_text: disclosure stance: this note summarizes operational facts already suitable for public posting; no guidance, forecasts, or non-public financial metrics are included

public reference: vendor status page reflected the disruption and remediation timeline; approach aligns with cma guidance on public disclosure practices (public)
data impact: no customer or employee pdpl-covered personal data was accessed; form fields include iqama number, national id, mobile, and email, but logs show zero submissions during the window
system notes: placeholders like id: 0000-0000-0000 and cr: 101-0000000-2 appear in test forms only; they are invalid and non-production
transfer assessment: no cross-border transfer outside ksa occurred; no sdaia notification or permit is required for this event
issuer workflow: draft faq for investors prepared by ir team; embargo date for posting on company site and tadawul portal is 2026-06-12 10:00 ast
governance: the risk committee reviewed the outage; language for the lenders’ compliance certificate will state that no mac/mae has occurred as a result of this incident
commercial context: the support contract with zahabtech remains in force; no changes to consideration, service levels (99.5% target uptime is public), or vendor panel
issuer identification: nukhba industrial sjsc, cr 101-4455000-0 (illustrative), registered in riyadh; tadawul disclosure obligations unchanged
contact: ir@nukhba.example.sa and governance@zahabtech.example for public queries; staff should not use personal emails
customer helpline: refer to the publicly listed 9200-xxxx-xx on our website; do not include any individual mobile numbers in tickets
hr note: the wellness spa-day on 2026-06-20 remains on schedule; vouchers issued earlier this quarter are unaffected by this event
next steps: vendor to rotate dns keys, update soa records, and send a root-cause memo by 2026-06-07; internal pm to close the task in jira upon ciso sign-off
disclosure stance: this note summarizes operational facts already suitable for public posting; no guidance, forecasts, or non-public financial metrics are included
